reviews.review_msg: Dr. Hugo Cardoso is one of the best professors I have had at SFU. The 5 credits can be intimidating, but he makes the course not feel so heavy. His grading scheme is set up for students to succeed. If you are thinking about taking this course with Dr. Cardoso, just do it!
Best class I have ever taken. I actually was excited to study for this class.

Dr. Cardoso is by far the best professor I have had in my 4 years at SFU. Do not let the 5 credits for this class scare you. If you put in the work, go to the labs, it is easy to understand the content. This was by far my favourite class I have ever taken. He also makes the grading so that you will get the best possible outcome.

He provides ways for his students to benefit the most and receive the highest grades possible. He has a grading curve allowing for flexible grading which best benefits the highest mark of the student. He is passionate and interested in student success. If you attend the labs you will be fine for the quizzes.

Great prof! It's been a very interesting class! The labs aren't super hard unless you're not going to them and open labs, which I recommend not missing! He also has an adjustable grading scheme so you can get the best final grade that works to your benefit. Read the text, go to lectures and labs, and you'll do well. I'm so glad I took this class :)

Dr. Cardoso & the TA this semester truly care about their students' well-being and success! Both were friendly and approachable - I would not hesitate to take another class with Dr. Cardoso. He offers an extremely flexible grading scheme and extra credit, as well as an open lab. Highly advise going to open lab - makes an enormous difference!

Such a good prof, genuinely cares about students + wants us to learn. Grading criteria is very clear and generous too. Lab quizzes are a bit tricky but go to open labs as much as you can + practice lots and you should be fine. The lab instructor/TA Mariana was amazing as well! very knowledgeable + helpful in labs. Def recommend taking this course!!

Best class I've ever taken, prof and TA were both amazing! He really cares about his students & is very fair. He offers a VERY flexible grading system & an extra credit!! Super approachable, knowledgeable and honest. Difficult class but go to labs and you'll be fine!

Hugo is a fantastic person and prof, the course is very challenging but go to every lab and open lab and you'll do well. If you are struggling with something TALK TO HIM, he's actually very approachable and kind. You have the option to submit your lab note folder for a grade boost. He really wants students to do well.

Hugo is an amazing prof, he is super knowledgeable and clearly passionate about osteology. He gets to know all of his students (makes sense bc it's so lab heavy) and has students participate in lecture. It's a pretty difficult course, you need to go to lab every week. Go to open labs as well, they will save you

well, Hugo. I have to give you a solid B for being an OK prof this term. For those of you who is taking this course, make sure you have a good partner for the project. I was solid A up untill the project. My partner refused to do her part and I suffered 2 grades bc of her poor performance. Otherwise, Hugo is fair, extremely thorough and critical.
Best prof. I have ever had. Really hard class but super fun.
Buy the lab manual. ATTEND open lab hours. Get your hands on those bones. Everything will make sense when you touch the bones with your hand and learn them with your heart.
amazing and articulate prof, cares about his students and the course content. make sure you attend all labs, as well as the extra lab time.

Lectures were painful. To be fair, the class was an easy-credit, everyone needs it kind of course, but the prof made no effort to make his material engaging - or if he did, he's a worse professor than even I suspect. Besides the easy A, avoid it at all costs. Unless you have a penchant for dull, jumbled, long, frankly embarrassing lectures.

Lectures were fairly dry, not much humor just got to the facts. 2 hours of lecture followed by an hour of video and discussion. Doesn't post notes prior to class. 2 tests worth 50% each, just study the lecture slides and you should be fine. Maybe 2-3 questions on each test from the videos. Also don't need to buy the book!

Good way to get a science credit. Lectures were dry but Hugo is knowledgable & encourages questions. Workload is light - grade is 50% midterm and 50% final (multiple choice). Could probably get away w/ not buying the book as tests are mostly based on lectures. Questions can be very specific but if you study the lecture slides you should do alright

Arch 131 was disappointing. 50% for each exam. He posts the slides on canvas so there is no initiative to go to class and he passes through the slides so quickly it's impossible to take notes. He seems like an ok prof but I'm not sure I would take a large class with him again.

Hugo is highly knowledgable, but unfortunately there is just way too much information to memorize for the midterm and final, and the worst part is 90% of what is covered in lecture isn't even on the exam. Taking notes is not essential, everything that is on the exams is on his lecture slides. He is available for help if you need it.

The lectures were long and his monotone voice made them even longer, but the videos were good. Slides were on the whole unhelpful, and he contradicts himself/gets confused in lecture. 50% midterm and 50% final with this poorly delivered (though objectively interesting) material just wasn't a good idea. Really disappointing course.
tough tests, but it's forgivable because he has a soul patch. his patch is 10/10. Course notes were not posted before the actual lecture.

Long lectures, but if you know how much you are interested in biological anthropology, please take this course. He is not the type to joke around but he is a nice guy and will help you if you ask for it. Reading the textbook is not necessary, but it will help to fulfill those small details he doesn't tend to cover in his slides. Both exams were MC.

Amazing prof! Super passionate about what he teaches and genuinely cares that he's teaching well and that students are enjoying and learning from his class. Makes the three-hour lecture enjoyable with many breaks and videos that are super interesting! Only thing that was slightly worrisome was 50% midterm, 50% final, but wasn't too difficult!
Overall good guy, who expects students to do well, but does not spoon feed.

Downright exceptional prof. Very knowledgable. Encourages participation and questions. Tough marker, but is clear about what he wants and delivers useful feedback. Lectures are long but very informative.

Be prepared to remember A LOT of features. Labs are super important, and going to open labs will help. Hugo is a good prof if you take the initiative to ask him questions or for help. He's knowledgeable and knows the material really well. I think he knows the class is hard, so he offers chances for easy marksextra credit.  tl;dr hard class, study

The lab quizzes are absolutely ridiculous. The student expectation for this class is through the roof. If you think you may be super interested in the class or it is a requirement, take it. However, take as few classes as possible in the same semester. If it is not required avoid this class like the plague.
Great prof, lots of information, couple of videos, good to attend as gives sample Final questions each lecture, good to go. LOTS to know for lab quizzes, lots of fragmented parts.

His lectures are interesting but will soon lost interest in it especially when it's 3 hours long and at 830am. The lab quizzes are easy when you study. fair game. overall gained a lot from this course, you will learn a lot and can use it back in real life!
